title: Cleome steveniana
taxonomy: Cleome steveniana is a species in the family Cleomaceae, commonly known as the spider flower or bee plant family. It belongs to the genus Cleome, which comprises about 170 species of flowering plants. Cleome steveniana is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is known for its showy, fragrant flowers and is often grown as an ornamental plant. It is a member of the order Brassicales, which includes plants such as mustard, broccoli, and cabbage.

intro: Cleome steveniana, also known as Steven's bee plant or spider flower, is a flowering plant species within the Cleomaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome steveniana include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
